title: Cassia unica
taxonomy: Cassia unica is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae, and it is commonly known as a tree. The classification of Cassia unica places it within the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, which is a group of plants that are known for their woody stems and compound leaves. This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, and Cassia unica is thought to be a member of the Cassieae tribe. The Cassieae tribe is a large and diverse group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Cassia unica's classification as a member of the Fabaceae family is supported by its characteristic legume fruit and its ability to fix nitrogen from the air.

intro: Cassia unica, also known as Single Cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Cassia unica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cassia unica is made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
